sourceMovie.title: Wonder Woman
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Wonder Woman for the same reason it broke through in 2017: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a adventure rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Patty Jenkins keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The action and adventure blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Tomb Raider
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Wonder Woman clicked for you, Tomb Raider (2018) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and adventure, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Justice League: Gods and Monsters
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Wonder Woman clicked for you, Justice League: Gods and Monsters (2015) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and adventure, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Wonder Woman?
faq.answer: Try Tomb Raider, Justice League: Gods and Monsters, Green Lantern: First Flight—each shares DNA with Wonder Woman while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Wonder Woman?
faq.answer: Wonder Woman (2017) is best described as Action, Adventure, Fantasy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Wonder Woman part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Wonder Woman Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
